title: Today This Scripture Is Fulfilled
subtitle: The Lord rises to read in the synagogue at Nazareth, and reveals that the prophet spoke of Him

stillness: Be still, and let the noise within you fall silent.
First we must sit and weep through the humility of penance;
then rise from our vices through serenity of conscience.
Be quiet now, and turn your eyes toward Him.
gospel: And He came to Nazareth, where He had been brought up.
And on the sabbath day He entered the synagogue, according to His custom,
and He rose up to read.
And the book of Isaiah the prophet was handed to Him.
And when He had unrolled the book, He found the place where it was written:
“The Spirit of the Lord is upon me;
because of this He has anointed me,
to bring good tidings to the poor He has sent me,
to heal the contrite of heart,
to set at liberty those who are broken,
to preach the acceptable year of the Lord.”
And the eyes of all in the synagogue were fixed upon Him.
And He began to say to them:
“Attend and understand, for today this Scripture is fulfilled in your ears” — that is, in myself.
behold: how, humbly taking up the office of reader,
with kindly and serene countenance He reads among them.
For as Ambrose says: “So did He devote Himself to every service,
that He did not even disdain the office of a reader.”
Behold also how, humbly revealing Himself, He says:
“Today this Scripture is fulfilled in your ears.”
As if He said: I am He of whom the prophet speaks.
See that the eyes of all were fixed intently upon Him,
for the power of His words, and His humble and comely face;
for in His face a certain radiance of divine grace shone forth,
which drew to itself the souls of those who beheld Him.
consider: He was most beautiful:
a virgin born of the Virgin,
not of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but born of God.
He was most eloquent also;
and of both it had been said to Him through the prophet:
“Beautiful in form beyond the sons of men;
grace is poured upon your lips.”
He did not preach Himself by His own word alone,
but by the testimony of sacred Scripture.
Not by chance, but of set purpose He found the place,
for He knew the passages of Scripture that spoke of Him.
Standing, He read, for while He opened the Scriptures to us,
He labored in the flesh;
but afterward He sat down, for He restored Himself to the rest on high.
And we are instructed here:
first to sit and weep through the humility of penance;
then to rise from vices through serenity of conscience;
third to read — that is, to do good well —
and afterward to take our rest,
awaiting the rewards of quiet,
that we may receive at last those things in which we shall rest forever.
prayer: who so humbled yourself to every service
that you did not disdain to take up the office of reader,
and at last, after the gift of your teaching humbly poured out upon the men of Nazareth,
you patiently received evil for good,
when you permitted yourself to be led without resistance
by those who wished to cast you down:
give me, good Jesus, this grace —
that, imitating you, I may long to bend myself to every service of humility,
and offer myself gladly and ready;
grant me also to bear injuries patiently,
and to seek no vengeance for them,
and to love all my adversaries from the heart,
and to show them kindness. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: In facie ejus quidam fulgor divinæ gratiæ relucebat, qui animos intuentium attrahebat.
latinJewel.english: In His face a certain radiance of divine grace shone forth, which drew to itself the souls of those who beheld Him.
